title: Biweekly change in confirmed COVID-19 cases
subtitle: The biweekly change on any given date measures the percentage change in the number of new confirmed cases over the last 14 days, relative to the number in the previous 14 days. Due to limited testing, the number of confirmed cases is lower than the true number of infections.
caption:
Biweekly change in confirmed COVID-19 cases, Feb 22, 2026

The biweekly change on any given date measures the percentage change in the number of new confirmed cases over the last 14 days, relative to the number in the previous 14 days. Due to limited testing, the number of confirmed cases is lower than the true number of infections.

This choropleth map displays the biweekly percentage change in confirmed COVID-19 cases across the globe as of February 22, 2026, using a diverging color scale where shades of blue represent a decrease in cases and shades of orange represent an increase. The legend defines the scale from a 100% decrease to a 100% increase, with hatched patterns indicating regions where no data is available. The majority of the world, including the United States, Canada, Russia, China, Australia, and the entirety of Africa, appears hatched, signifying a lack of reported data for this period. Among the countries with visible data, the most significant declines are concentrated in South America and parts of Europe. Peru and Guatemala are shaded in the darkest blue, placing them in the -75% to -100% band, indicating the steepest drops in case numbers. Uruguay and several Balkan nations, including Albania and North Macedonia, fall into the -50% to -75% range, shown in a medium blue. In contrast, positive trends are visible in a smaller cluster of nations. Brazil and Sweden are colored in light orange, corresponding to the 0% to 25% band, showing a slight rise in infections. Estonia appears in a darker orange shade, placing it in the 25% to 50% category. The visual distribution shows that where data exists, negative changes in case counts are more widespread and often of greater magnitude than positive changes.
